metadata.title: Why Students Who Practice With AI Score Lower on the Exam
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut for claude-for-education-a-practitioners-guide candidate 05. Source chapter: claude-for-education-a-practitioners-guide/chapters/97-fundamental-themes.md (frictional theme only — Bastani study, Kosmyna EEG, phase gate).
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock

style_bible.visual_style: editorial paper on newsprint; flat annotation plane; serif labels with hairline underlines
style_bible.color_palette: cream ground, charcoal serif type, TEAL = genuine learning / engaged brain, CRIMSON = borrowed cognition / score collapse

metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = cognitive friction present / genuine learning / exam success; CRIMSON = friction removed / borrowed cognition / score collapse; never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Card exclusions honored: NO neuroscience terminology beyond dopamine/synapse; NO full Kosmyna EEG study detail; NO tier taxonomy enumeration; do not discuss BDNF by name. Core mechanism only: AI-assisted practice removes friction → removes trigger → no consolidation → exam collapse.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: NO RENDERABLE BEATS: no beat carries a shot.remotion.pattern or Manim annotation — the pipeline will render NOTHING and every slot becomes a YOU-slate. The sheet was authored without the scene contract.
B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B07: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Students who practiced with AI scored 48 percent higher during practice — and 17 percentage points lower on the actual exam.
beats.card_text:
48 % higher during practice.
17 pp lower on the exam.
beats.notes: CARD: two numbers — TEAL 48%, CRIMSON 17pp. The tension is the hook.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. Students who practiced with AI scored 48 percent higher during practice — and 17 percentage points lower on the actual exam.

beats.narration_text: More successful practice should predict better exam performance. Here is the study where it predicted worse. Why?
beats.card_text:
More practice success
should mean better exams.
It didn't. Why?
beats.notes: THE QUESTION beat — named on screen and in narration.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: More successful practice should predict better exam performance. Here is the study where it predicted worse. Why?

beats.narration_text: The Bastani study gave one group of students unrestricted AI access during math practice. Their practice scores were excellent. When the same exam was given without AI, they scored dramatically below students who had practiced the hard way.
beats.notes: Two learning curves: AI-assisted shoots up steeply (CRIMSON), then collapses on exam day; control group climbs modestly (TEAL), holds on exam. The visual is the argument.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The Bastani study gave one group of students unrestricted AI access during math practice. Their practice scores were excellent. When the same exam was given wit

beats.narration_text: Here is why. When the brain meets something it cannot yet do, a prediction error fires. Dopamine releases. Synapses strengthen. That chain of events is memory consolidation. Remove the struggle — and you remove the trigger.
beats.notes: Chain: STRUGGLE → PREDICTION ERROR → DOPAMINE → SYNAPSE STRENGTH → MEMORY. TEAL chain. Then second chain: AI REMOVES STRUGGLE → NO TRIGGER → NO CONSOLIDATION. CRIMSON chain.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Here is why. When the brain meets something it cannot yet do, a prediction error fires. Dopamine releases. Synapses strengthen. That chain of events is memory c

beats.narration_text: Cognitive struggle is not the price of learning. It is the mechanism of learning. AI that removes the struggle removes the mechanism.
beats.card_text:
Struggle is not the price
of learning.
It is the mechanism.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Cognitive struggle is not the price of learning. It is the mechanism of learning. AI that removes the struggle removes the mechanism.

beats.narration_text: The fix is a phase gate. AI handles the scaffolding — finding sources, structuring problems, calibrating feedback. The student handles the thinking. The gate is at the point where cognitive struggle is the learning.
beats.notes: Horizontal bar split: AI SIDE (TEAL, left) — scaffolding, sources, feedback. Gate line in center. STUDENT SIDE (TEAL, right) — synthesis, argument, explanation. Gate label: 'struggle is here'.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The fix is a phase gate. AI handles the scaffolding — finding sources, structuring problems, calibrating feedback. The student handles the thinking. The gate is

beats.narration_text: AI that makes the struggle more productive accelerates learning. AI that replaces the struggle removes it.
beats.card_text:
Make the struggle more productive.
Don't remove it.
beats.notes: Recap endcard. AI IN EDUCATION topic kicker.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: AI that makes the struggle more productive accelerates learning. AI that replaces the struggle removes it.
